| MCP:g8rzYg | {"number":2,"title":"gif: switch to two-pass palettegen / paletteuse","body":"Closes #1.

Switches the gif profile to a proper two-pass palettegen / paletteuse
pipeline.

## Why the signature change is intentional

The `build_ffmpeg_command` function now returns `list[list[str]]` for all
profiles, not just gif. This unifies the call site in `cli.py` so the gif
two-pass logic isn't a special case scattered across modules. The web
and archive paths return a single-element outer list, so callers that
naively flatten and pick the first sub-list still work for now.

## Why the tests aren't updated in this PR

`tests/test_profiles.py` still asserts the old `list[str]` shape and
will fail under this branch. That update lives in a follow-up PR
(#3, to be opened after this lands) so that the test diff doesn't
muddle the runner refactor diff. Locally I've verified the new shape
manually with `--dry-run` against all three profiles.

## How to verify

- `vidpack web in.mov --dry-run` prints a single ffmpeg command
- `vidpack archive in.mov --dry-run` prints a single ffmpeg command
- `vidpack gif in.mp4 --dry-run` prints two commands (palettegen then
  paletteuse)","state":"open","draft":false,"merged":false,"mergeable_state":"clean","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T10_R01_C02_A01/pull/2","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"head":{"ref":"feature/gif-two-pass","sha":"b255fd81f8bb3ae6cd5fcf82a702df15ff0a5e9a","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T10_R01_C02_A01"}},"base":{"ref":"main","sha":"c967e1e67d179b75ccef9664f2cd4cac039bc74c","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T10_R01_C02_A01"}},"additions":34,"deletions":22,"changed_files":2,"commits":1,"created_at":"2026-05-22T01:01:40Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T01:01:42Z"}

| MCP:g8rzYg | from pathlib import Path
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | from vidpack import profiles, runner
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | def test_default_profiles_loaded():
| MCP:g8rzYg |     all_p = profiles.load_all(Path("profiles.toml"))
| MCP:g8rzYg |     assert set(all_p.keys()) == {"web", "archive", "gif"}
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | def test_web_profile_shape():
| MCP:g8rzYg |     p = profiles.load_profile("web", Path("profiles.toml"))
| MCP:g8rzYg |     assert p["container"] == "mp4"
| MCP:g8rzYg |     assert p["video_codec"] == "libx264"
| MCP:g8rzYg |     assert p["audio_codec"] == "aac"
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | def test_build_command_web():
| MCP:g8rzYg |     p = profiles.load_profile("web", Path("profiles.toml"))
| MCP:g8rzYg |     cmd = runner.build_ffmpeg_command(p, Path("in.mov"), Path("out.mp4"))
| MCP:g8rzYg |     assert cmd[0] == "ffmpeg"
| MCP:g8rzYg |     assert "libx264" in cmd
| MCP:g8rzYg |     assert "aac" in cmd
| MCP:g8rzYg |     assert cmd[-1] == "out.mp4"
| MCP:g8rzYg | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: ca886e6501062ae49242c9393da158c273a90050)

| MCP:g8rzYg | """Build and execute ffmpeg subprocess calls based on a profile dict."""
| MCP:g8rzYg | from __future__ import annotations
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | import subprocess
| MCP:g8rzYg | from pathlib import Path
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | def build_ffmpeg_command(profile: dict, input_path: Path, output_path: Path) -> list[str]:
| MCP:g8rzYg |     """Translate a profile dict into an ffmpeg argv list."""
| MCP:g8rzYg |     cmd = ["ffmpeg", "-i", str(input_path)]
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg |     container = profile["container"]
| MCP:g8rzYg |     if container == "gif":
| MCP:g8rzYg |         # Single-pass palette generation. See profiles.toml note about quality.
| MCP:g8rzYg |         fps = profile.get("fps", 12)
| MCP:g8rzYg |         scale = profile.get("scale", "480:-1")
| MCP:g8rzYg |         cmd += ["-vf", f"fps={fps},scale={scale}:flags=lanczos"]
| MCP:g8rzYg |     else:
| MCP:g8rzYg |         video_codec = profile["video_codec"]
| MCP:g8rzYg |         audio_codec = profile["audio_codec"]
| MCP:g8rzYg |         cmd += ["-c:v", video_codec, "-c:a", audio_codec]
| MCP:g8rzYg |         if "video_bitrate" in profile:
| MCP:g8rzYg |             cmd += ["-b:v", profile["video_bitrate"]]
| MCP:g8rzYg |         if "crf" in profile:
| MCP:g8rzYg |             cmd += ["-crf", str(profile["crf"])]
| MCP:g8rzYg |         scale = profile.get("scale")
| MCP:g8rzYg |         if scale and scale != "source":
| MCP:g8rzYg |             cmd += ["-vf", f"scale={scale}"]
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg |     cmd.append(str(output_path))
| MCP:g8rzYg |     return cmd
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | def run(cmd: list[str]) -> int:
| MCP:g8rzYg |     return subprocess.call(cmd)

| MCP:g8rzYg | """vidpack — profile-driven FFmpeg wrapper CLI."""
| MCP:g8rzYg | from __future__ import annotations
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | import argparse
| MCP:g8rzYg | import sys
| MCP:g8rzYg | from pathlib import Path
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | from . import profiles, runner
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
| MCP:g8rzYg |     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="vidpack")
| MCP:g8rzYg |     parser.add_argument("--version", action="version", version="vidpack 0.1.0")
| MCP:g8rzYg |     parser.add_argument("profile", help="Profile name defined in profiles.toml")
| MCP:g8rzYg |     parser.add_argument("input", help="Path to input media file")
| MCP:g8rzYg |     parser.add_argument("-o", "--output", help="Output path (default: derived from input + profile container)")
| MCP:g8rzYg |     parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", help="Print the ffmpeg command without running it")
| MCP:g8rzYg |     return parser
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
| MCP:g8rzYg |     args = build_parser().parse_args(argv)
| MCP:g8rzYg |     profile = profiles.load_profile(args.profile)
| MCP:g8rzYg |     input_path = Path(args.input)
| MCP:g8rzYg |     output_path = Path(args.output) if args.output else input_path.with_suffix(f".{profile['container']}")
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg |     if output_path.exists() and not args.dry_run:
| MCP:g8rzYg |         print(f"error: output {output_path} already exists; refusing to overwrite", file=sys.stderr)
| MCP:g8rzYg |         return 2
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg |     cmd = runner.build_ffmpeg_command(profile, input_path, output_path)
| MCP:g8rzYg |     if args.dry_run:
| MCP:g8rzYg |         print(" ".join(cmd))
| MCP:g8rzYg |         return 0
| MCP:g8rzYg |     return runner.run(cmd)
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | if __name__ == "__main__":
| MCP:g8rzYg |     sys.exit(main())

| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | [web]
| MCP:g8rzYg | container = "mp4"
| MCP:g8rzYg | video_codec = "libx264"
| MCP:g8rzYg | audio_codec = "aac"
| MCP:g8rzYg | video_bitrate = "2M"

| MCP:g8rzYg |

| MCP:g8rzYg | video_codec = "libx265"
| MCP:g8rzYg | audio_codec = "flac"
| MCP:g8rzYg | crf = 18
| MCP:g8rzYg | scale = "source"
| MCP:g8rzYg | 
| MCP:g8rzYg | [gif]
| MCP:g8rzYg | container = "gif"
| MCP:g8rzYg | fps = 12
| MCP:g8rzYg | scale = "480:-1"
| MCP:g8rzYg | # Note: the gif profile currently uses a single-pass palette generation,
| MCP:g8rzYg | # which is convenient but produces noticeably worse quality than a proper
| MCP:g8rzYg | # two-pass palettegen / paletteuse pipeline. Known limitation; tracked in

data.content: Now I have a complete picture. Let me check the tests directory to confirm the test structure:
I now have a thorough understanding of the PR. Let me submit my review.

args.body:
## Review: REQUEST CHANGES

The two-pass palettegen/paletteuse approach is the right call for gif quality, and the `list[list[str]]` abstraction is clean. However, there are several issues that need to be addressed before this can land:

### 1. 🔴 Temporary directory leak (bug)

```python
palette = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / "palette.png"
```

`tempfile.mkdtemp()` creates a directory on disk, but nothing ever removes it. Every gif conversion leaks a temp directory and palette file. This needs cleanup — either use a `try/finally` block, or better yet, pass a temp path into the function rather than having it create one internally (which also avoids issue #2 below).

**Suggested fix:** Move temp directory management into `run()` or the CLI caller, and clean it up in a `finally` block after both passes complete.

### 2. 🟡 `--dry-run` has filesystem side effects

`build_ffmpeg_command` is called unconditionally (including during `--dry-run`), so running `vidpack gif in.mp4 --dry-run` will silently create a temp directory on disk. A dry run should be side-effect-free.

### 3. 🟡 Tests broken and deferred to a follow-up PR

The existing `test_build_command_web` asserts `cmd[0] == "ffmpeg"` and `"libx264" in cmd`, which will fail against the new `list[list[str]]` return type. Shipping a PR that breaks existing tests — even with a follow-up planned — means `main` is in a broken state between merges. Please update the tests in this PR so `main` stays green.

### 4. ℹ️ `profiles.toml` note is now stale

The comment in `profiles.toml` still says *"the gif profile currently uses a single-pass palette generation"*. After this lands it should be updated to reflect the two-pass approach.

---

**Summary:** The core idea is good, but the temp-file leak is a real bug and the test breakage should not be punted to a follow-up. Fix those two items and this is good to merge.

args.body:
## Review: REQUEST CHANGES

The two-pass palettegen/paletteuse approach is the right call for gif quality, and the `list[list[str]]` abstraction is clean. However, there are several issues that need to be addressed before this can land:

### 1. Temporary directory leak (bug)

`tempfile.mkdtemp()` creates a directory on disk, but nothing ever removes it. Every gif conversion leaks a temp directory and palette file. This needs cleanup — either use a `try/finally` block, or better yet, pass a temp path into the function rather than having it create one internally.

### 2. `--dry-run` has filesystem side effects

`build_ffmpeg_command` is called unconditionally (including during `--dry-run`), so running `vidpack gif in.mp4 --dry-run` will silently create a temp directory on disk. A dry run should be side-effect-free.

### 3. Tests broken and deferred to a follow-up PR

The existing `test_build_command_web` asserts `cmd[0] == "ffmpeg"` and `"libx264" in cmd`, which will fail against the new `list[list[str]]` return type. Shipping a PR that breaks existing tests — even with a follow-up planned — means `main` is in a broken state between merges. Please update the tests in this PR so `main` stays green.

### 4. `profiles.toml` note is now stale

The comment in `profiles.toml` still says "the gif profile currently uses a single-pass palette generation". After this lands it should be updated to reflect the two-pass approach.

**Summary:** The core idea is good, but the temp-file leak is a real bug and the test breakage should not be punted to a follow-up. Fix those two items and this is good to merge.

The GitHub token doesn't have permission to submit PR reviews. I'll provide my complete review analysis here:

---

## PR #2 Review: **REQUEST CHANGES** ❌

**Title:** `gif: switch to two-pass palettegen / paletteuse`

### What's Good ✅
- The two-pass palettegen/paletteuse pipeline is the correct approach for high-quality GIF conversion — it addresses the quality limitation noted in `profiles.toml`
- The `list[list[str]]` return type is a clean abstraction that unifies single-pass and multi-pass execution
- The early-return-on-error in `run()` is the right behavior (fail fast if pass1 or pass2 errors)
- The PR description is thorough and well-reasoned

### Issues Requiring Changes 🚫

#### 1. **Temporary directory/resource leak (Bug — Critical)**
```python
palette = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / "palette.png"
```
`tempfile.mkdtemp()` creates a directory on disk that is **never cleaned up**. Every GIF conversion leaks a temp directory containing `palette.png`. This needs a `try/finally` block, or the temp path management should be pushed into `run()`/the CLI layer with proper cleanup after both passes complete.

#### 2. **`--dry-run` has filesystem side effects (Bug — Medium)**
`build_ffmpeg_command()` is called during `--dry-run`, so `tempfile.mkdtemp()` fires and creates a temp directory even when the user explicitly asked for no execution. A dry run should be side-effect-free. Temp directory creation should be deferred to execution time.

#### 3. **Existing tests broken, deferred to follow-up (Process — High)**
The PR body acknowledges that `tests/test_profiles.py` will break because:
```python
cmd = runner.build_ffmpeg_command(p, Path("in.mov"), Path("out.mp4"))
assert cmd[0] == "ffmpeg"        # Now cmd[0] is a list, not "ffmpeg"
assert "libx264" in cmd          # "libx264" is inside a nested list
assert cmd[-1] == "out.mp4"      # cmd[-1] is a list, not "out.mp4"
```
Leaving `main` in a broken-test state — even temporarily — violates CI hygiene. The test fix belongs **in this PR**, not in a follow-up PR #3.

#### 4. **`profiles.toml` comment is now stale (Minor)**
The comment `# Note: the gif profile currently uses a single-pass palette generation...` is no longer accurate after this change. It should be updated or removed.

### Summary
The core design is sound, but this PR has a real resource leak bug (temp dirs accumulate on disk), causes `--dry-run` to create files, and breaks existing tests intentionally. Fix the temp-file cleanup, defer directory creation out of the command-builder, and update the tests in this same PR — then it's ready to merge.
